model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Louisville holds a significant edge in predictive metrics, ranking 16th-18th nationally with an adjusted efficiency margin of +25.42 compared to South Florida's +15.77 at 50th, reflecting advantages on both ends of the floor (AdjO 124.1 vs 117.7, AdjD 98.7 vs 101.9). The key uncertainty is star point guard Mikel Brown Jr.'s questionable status due to a back injury—Louisville went just 7-5 without him—which tempers confidence and makes this closer than the raw numbers suggest. South Florida enters on an 11-game winning streak with both AAC regular season and tournament titles, giving them legitimate upset potential as a well-coached, confident team, but the roughly 10-point efficiency gap and Louisville's ACC-tested depth should be enough to prevail even at reduced strength, consistent with the 4.5-point spread.

reasoningStep.title: East 6/11: Louisville vs South Florida
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville's superior metrics and talent edge overcome South Florida's momentum, though the Mikel Brown Jr. injury concern keeps this from being a high-confidence pick.

result.text: Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr. for March Madness seed Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury for March Madness seed C.L. Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC Tournament cost UofL from having a better seed and a slightly easier path to advancing. Brown has done everything he can to play, even trying to play hurt through the pain. After he did so in UofL's loss to Clemson, coach Pat Kelsey said it was best to shut him down until he could come back fully healthy. Brown told The Courier Journal after Louisville's ACC Tournament win over SMU that it's been a childhood dream to play in the NCAA Tournament, which he called "the best basketball (atmosphere) in the world." "I'm not going to just sit out for no reason," Brown said. "I want to play with these guys, I want to be out there, I want to play for the team, but my health is first." Brown's absence was costly in terms of the hypothetical games the Cards perhaps could have won had he played. And it was costly in terms of the reality that his status was a topic of conversation by the NCAA Selection Committee when it began constructing the bracket. Latest on Louisville Star’s Injury Status for March Madness Tournament Opener Mikel Brown Jr. is questionable for Louisville's NCAA Tournament opener as Pat Kelsey reports encouraging progress on the star's back. March 17, 2026 | 3:05 PM EDT Mikel Brown Jr. hasn’t played a basketball game in nearly three weeks. Louisville’s star freshman guard, a projected lottery pick who tied Wes Unseld’s program scoring record with 45 points against NC State last month, has been reduced to watching his teammates practice while nursing the back injury that’s defined his first college season. Now, with the Cardinals facing No. 11 seed South Florida on Thursday in the NCAA Tournament’s first round, Brown’s status remains officially questionable. Louisville coach Pat Kelsey offered an encouraging update Monday, telling reporters, “He made great progress last week. We’ll see how the next couple days of practice go.” The Cardinals (23-10) need Brown in the lineup if they’re going to make a deep run. Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr. for March Madness seed Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury for March Madness seed C.L. Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC Tournament cost UofL from having a better seed and a slightly easier path to advancing. Brown has done everything he can to play, even trying to play hurt through the pain. After he did so in UofL's loss to Clemson, coach Pat Kelsey said it was best to shut him down until he could come back fully healthy. Brown told The Courier Journal after Louisville's ACC Tournament win over SMU that it's been a childhood dream to play in the NCAA Tournament, which he called "the best basketball (atmosphere) in the world." "I'm not going to just sit out for no reason," Brown said. "I want to play with these guys, I want to be out there, I want to play for the team, but my health is first." Brown's absence was costly in terms of the hypothetical games the Cards perhaps could have won had he played. And it was costly in terms of the reality that his status was a topic of conversation by the NCAA Selection Committee when it began constructing the bracket. Latest on Louisville Star’s Injury Status for March Madness Tournament Opener Mikel Brown Jr. is questionable for Louisville's NCAA Tournament opener as Pat Kelsey reports encouraging progress on the star's back. March 17, 2026 | 3:05 PM EDT Mikel Brown Jr. hasn’t played a basketball game in nearly three weeks. Louisville’s star freshman guard, a projected lottery pick who tied Wes Unseld’s program scoring record with 45 points against NC State last month, has been reduced to watching his teammates practice while nursing the back injury that’s defined his first college season. Now, with the Cardinals facing No. 11 seed South Florida on Thursday in the NCAA Tournament’s first round, Brown’s status remains officially questionable. Louisville coach Pat Kelsey offered an encouraging update Monday, telling reporters, “He made great progress last week. We’ll see how the next couple days of practice go.” The Cardinals (23-10) need Brown in the lineup if they’re going to make a deep run. Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile my analysis:

**Key findings:**

1. **Ratings gap:** Louisville ranks 16th (Torvik) / 18th (KenPom) with an AdjEM of +25.42 and elite offense (AdjO 124.1, 20th) and strong defense (AdjD 98.7). South Florida ranks 50th in both systems with AdjEM of +15.77, AdjO 117.7 (58th), AdjD 101.9 (60th). That's roughly a 10-point efficiency margin gap.

2. **Mikel Brown Jr. injury:** Louisville's star PG has a back injury and is listed as questionable. Louisville went 7-5 without him. This is a significant concern but reports suggest "encouraging progress."

3. **South Florida momentum:** USF has won 11 straight, won both the AAC regular season and tournament titles, and is 25-8. This is their first NCAA Tournament since 2012.

4. **Odds:** Louisville is a 4.5-point favorite with -200 moneyline.

5. **6 vs 11 seed:** Historically, 6-seeds win about 63% of the time against 11-seeds, making this one of the more upset-prone lines.

Even with the Brown Jr. injury concern, Louisville's talent and metrics advantage is substantial. The 10-point efficiency margin gap is significant, and even Louisville's 7-5 record without Brown suggests they can still compete. USF is a quality 11-seed with great momentum, but the AAC schedule doesn't fully prepare them for Louisville's ACC-tested roster.
